top_findings.title: MCP server cannot start — server.run_stdio() is not a real SDK method
top_findings.evidence: server.py:451 calls asyncio.run(server.run_stdio()); the mcp SDK Server class has no run_stdio(). stdio_server is imported at server.py:8 but never used. Correct pattern is `async with stdio_server() as (r,w): await server.run(r,w,server.create_initialization_options())`. main() raises AttributeError immediately.
top_findings.suggestion: Wire main() through the imported stdio_server() context manager and call server.run(read, write, init_options).

top_findings.title: get_team_stats W/L/D counts are wrong (double-counted from both perspectives)
top_findings.evidence: repository.py:195-197 sets wins = home_wins + away_wins, draws = home_draws + away_draws, losses = home_losses + away_losses. For any match set, home_wins counts home_goal>away_goal and away_wins counts away_goal>home_goal, so wins sums decisive results from BOTH sides — inflating wins/losses and reporting every decisive match as both a win and a loss; draws are also doubled. goals_for/goals_against (repository.py:182-183) always sum home_goal/away_goal regardless of whether the team played away, so away-match goals are attributed to the wrong side.
top_findings.suggestion: Split each match by whether the team was home or away and tally results/goals from that team's perspective; do not add the two opposite-perspective tallies together.
